rulings.Case Title: MAYNARD GARCIA et al VS. PEAK ATTRACTIONS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012, Line 17, DEFENDANTs REBOUND ACTION SPORTS, INC., KEVIN ROHWER's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Denied. Defendants assumed liability for amusement rides built and sold by S&S Sports when they purchased the company. Defendants were aware of the injury causing ride in California and continued to sell replacement parts for that ride. rulings.Case Title: MARIANNE CUNNINGHAM et al VS. GOLDEN GATE HEALTH CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And For Sanctions By Deft And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT KINDRED NURSING CENTER WEST LLC DBA GOLDEN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And For Sanctions By Deft And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. The interrogatory responses of Plaintiffs Marianne Cunningham and Richard Cunningham served prior to the filing of the motion were inadequate. The declaration of plaintiff's new counsel, Shawn R. Miller, states that plaintiffs have subsequently provided further responses. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to discuss their positions on whether the supplemental responses are adequate.

rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing By Defts For Motion To Compel Answers To Discovery And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT CELIA MORENO, COUNTY OF SAN MATEO Notice Of Hearing By Defts For Motion To Compel Answers To Discovery And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Plaintiff's responses to Form Interrogatory Nos. 210.5, 212.5, 213.1 and Special Interrogatory Nos. 4, 10, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 20, and Requests for Production Nos. 1, 2, 4, and 19 made prior to the filing of the motion were inadequate. The Declaration of Plaintiff's Attorney Daniel Ray Bacon states that plaintiff provided further responses to interrogatories and produced additional financial records on June 29th and July 3rd. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to discuss their positions on whether the supplemental responses and production are adequate =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: JAMES W. GAY VS. CREATIVE ALLIANCE, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Wb Coyle, Telegraph Hill Properties Inc., Danilo Design Group, L.P.'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 14, 2012, Line 4, Defendants Wb Coyle, Telegraph Hill Properties Inc., Danilo Design Group, L.P.'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address the following issues: (1) the parties' position on whether, assuming arguendo, that Ms. Thomson obtained the hard drive in violation of her duties to her former employer, the violation of her duty justifies quashing the third party subpoena; (2) Defendants should be prepared to provide a list of the people who Defendants contend should be notified if production of the hard-drive is ordered; and (3) what conditions should be placed upon the review of the hard drive by plaintiff's counsel in the event the subpoena is not quashed. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL RAPPAPORT, AS AN INDIVIDUAL, AND ON VS. JAMBA JUICE CO., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANT JAMBA JUICE CO.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. There is no indication from the face of the Complaint that Plaintiff attempts to assert a cause of action under the FTCA or BP § 17508 for which there is no private right of action. The Complaint is pled with the requisite specificity. For purposes of a UCL action such as this one where the unfair practice is a fraudulent advertising campaign, Plaintiff must demonstrate actual reliance on the allegedly deceptive or misleading statements but does need to plead or prove to an unrealistic degree of specificity that he relied on particular advertisements or statements. (See Kwikset Corp. v. Superior Court (2011) 51 Cal.4th 310, 326-37; In re Tobacco II Cases (2009) 46 Cal.4th 298, 328.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DUSTIN K. HENDERSON VS. BIOMATRIX INC. DBA BIOMATRIX INTERNATIONAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, August 14, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT BIOMATRIX, INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Denied. The portion of the Rutter Group cited by Defendant, when read in context, does not stand for the proposition that a choice-of-law clause is the same thing as a forum-selection clause and there is no legal authority for this position. There is no forum-selection clause between the parties. Plaintiff has presented evidence sufficient to establish specific personal jurisdiction over Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
